channel.title: Personal Portfolio Site
channel.description:
Hi guys!
I have built (still work in progress) a very simple portfolio site in Frontity. At the moment it's just a really simple 2 page site using ACF flexible content. I'm probably going to add a work and blog pages later.
Live: https://benwrightdev.com/ Code: https://github.com/ben-wright/ben-frontity
It's pretty quick! ![Screenshot 2020-06-12 at 08.17.23|690x440, 50%](upload://dNhHdIsu6MxetqLz0AN4B3rYhVL.png) ![Screenshot 2020-06-12 at 08.17.15|690x397, 50%](upload://kX2vKXwLd91gYmmZgB6gEIiifZS.png)
Experience with Frontity has been very good, I haven't had to dive deep into much functionality yet because it's a very simple site but everything was super easy to get started and the docs are comprehensive.
The only problem I experienced is when I deployed the site, I forgot swapping my domain from the wordpress instance to Frontity meant that the wp-source plugin would break! Instructions on how to setup a subdomain in vercel might be worth mentioning in the deployment section. I've now got wp.benwrightdev.com for the wordpress source.
Thanks for everyones awesome work on the framework! It's exciting and I'm definitely going to stick around and hopefully make some contributions!
Ben

item.title: Personal Portfolio Site
item.dc:creator: areebajaved270
item.description:
Absolutely, I concur. I’ve incorporated structured data and schemas corresponding to each post and blog on my O-level epastpaper site. It functions effectively and yields favorable outcomes.

Read full topic

item.title: Personal Portfolio Site
item.dc:creator: lockysaima
item.description:
Great job buddy. Also try to optimize its core web vitals. Then you will see its results will be more amazing. Because google loved structured data site. Also add all kind of schemas on your site. It will give you rocket kind a boost. Good luck.

Read full topic

item.title: Personal Portfolio Site
item.dc:creator: popat.jitendra
item.description:
Simple but nice one, great job Keep it up

I am trying my version soon #love #frontity

Read full topic

item.title: Personal Portfolio Site
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
Hey Ben!

I’m really glad to finally see your project live! It looks great! Thank you for sharing it and for giving us feedback, we really appreciate it!

If you do not mind, it will be featured in the next Frontity Newsletter and in the Showcase page.

Read full topic

item.title: Personal Portfolio Site
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Hi @ben.wright.dev

Thanks for sharing your site made with Frontity!

ben.wright.dev:

The only problem I experienced is when I deployed the site, I forgot swapping my domain from the wordpress instance to Frontity meant that the wp-source plugin would break! Instructions on how to setup a subdomain in vercel might be worth mentioning in the deployment section. I’ve now got wp.benwrightdev.com for the wordpress source.

Regarding this, we have created an issue to have a look at it and try to provide a better explanation on this → https://github.com/frontity/gitbook-docs/issues/98

Feel free to comment on the issue to add any specific information you missed from the docs so we can take it into account when we work on this.

Thanks a lot for your feedback

Read full topic

item.title: Personal Portfolio Site
item.dc:creator: ben.wright.dev
item.description:
Hi guys!

I have built (still work in progress) a very simple portfolio site in Frontity. At the moment it’s just a really simple 2 page site using ACF flexible content. I’m probably going to add a work and blog pages later.

Live:
https://benwrightdev.com/
Code:

GitHub

ben-wright/ben-frontity

Contribute to ben-wright/ben-frontity development by creating an account on GitHub.

It’s pretty quick!

Experience with Frontity has been very good, I haven’t had to dive deep into much functionality yet because it’s a very simple site but everything was super easy to get started and the docs are comprehensive.

The only problem I experienced is when I deployed the site, I forgot swapping my domain from the wordpress instance to Frontity meant that the wp-source plugin would break! Instructions on how to setup a subdomain in vercel might be worth mentioning in the deployment section. I’ve now got wp.benwrightdev.com for the wordpress source.

Thanks for everyones awesome work on the framework! It’s exciting and I’m definitely going to stick around and hopefully make some contributions!

Ben

Read full topic
